docs.summary: The onboarding hub: how new users move from signup to activation, the first-run checklist, and the retention loops we lean on.

section_summaries.heading: Activation funnel
section_summaries.summary: Signup to first value in four steps; the biggest drop is at email verification.

section_summaries.heading: First-run checklist
section_summaries.summary: Six tasks shown on first login; completing three predicts week-1 retention.

section_summaries.heading: Retention loops
section_summaries.summary: Two habit loops: the weekly digest and the collaboration nudge.

glossary_terms.definition: A new user reaching first value (completing 3 first-run tasks).
A recurring trigger that brings a user back without a new acquisition cost.

docs.body_md:
# Activation funnel

New users move from signup to first value in four steps. The biggest drop-off is at **email verification** (~28%).

## First-run checklist

Six tasks appear on first login. Completing three predicts week-1 retention.

# Retention loops

We lean on two habit loops: the weekly digest and the collaboration nudge.

quote_anchor.text: The biggest drop-off is at email verification

thread.author: maneesh
thread.text: Is this still true after the magic-link rollout?

docs.summary: Pricing experiments for 2026: the Q1 packaging test moved conversion +6%; Q2 explores annual-default and usage tiers.

section_summaries.heading: Q1 results
section_summaries.summary: Three-tier packaging beat the two-tier control by 6% on paid conversion.

section_summaries.heading: Q2 hypotheses
section_summaries.summary: Annual-as-default and a usage-based add-on tier are the two bets.

glossary_terms.definition: Presenting the annual plan pre-selected at checkout.

docs.body_md:
# Q1 results

The three-tier packaging test beat the two-tier control by **6%** on paid conversion.

# Q2 hypotheses

We are testing annual-as-default and a usage-based add-on tier.
